domain: Aikido tenkan technique (Japanese martial art): defender PIVOTS 180° to redirect attacker's momentum along curved path; uses attacker's force as energy source; tai-sabaki body-evasion with irimi entry + tenkan turning.
specific_mechanism: Redirect-not-block via tangent rotation about fixed pivot; accept incoming momentum vector and add perpendicular rotation converting linear attack to controlled circle.
llm_application: AIKIDO-REDIRECT adversarial momentum redirection at inference: for each input embedding x_t, compute deviation δ_t = x_t - Proj_M(x_t) from calibration manifold M (low-rank PCA reference). Apply ROTATION R(90°) about an axis in orthogonal subspace U⊥M to produce δ_t' = R(δ_t). Set output x_t' = Proj_M(x_t) + δ_t'. The adversarial component is rotated 90° into a tangent direction harmless to task-relevant manifold. Differs from (a) SmoothLLM input smoothing, (b) PGD adversarial training, (c) JailbreakRadar detection-only, (d) FLOAT-GLASS null-space attenuation (this batch — DAMPING vs ROTATION), (e) Self-Refine post-hoc by combining (i) DEVIATION from calibration manifold + (ii) 90-DEGREE ROTATION in U⊥M + (iii) INFERENCE-TIME redirection.
novelty_claim: PGD adversarial training. SmoothLLM smoothing. JailbreakRadar detection. FLOAT-GLASS null-space damping. AIKIDO-REDIRECT recombines (a) DEVIATION computation from manifold + (b) 90-DEGREE ROTATION instead of damping + (c) INFERENCE-TIME redirection. Three known components combined; rotation-instead-of-attenuation is the new combination angle.
